name: Replit
description: Browser IDE with AI agents for building apps
longDescription:
Replit is a browser-based development environment that lets you write, run, and host code without installing anything locally. Each project, called a Repl, runs in a Linux container with an editor, terminal, package manager, and live preview already wired up.

It supports dozens of languages including Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, and Rust, and can serve web apps directly from the editor. Replit's AI features, including the Agent and Assistant, can scaffold projects, edit files, and debug code from natural-language prompts.

The platform is widely used for learning to code, prototyping side projects, running classroom exercises, and shipping small web apps. Multiplayer editing lets several people work in the same Repl in real time, similar to a Google Doc.

features.title: Instant cloud IDE
features.description: Open a full Linux dev environment in seconds with editor, terminal, and preview built in.

features.title: AI Agent and Assistant
features.description: Natural-language coding tools scaffold apps, edit files, and explain or debug existing code.

features.title: Multiplayer editing
features.description: Multiple people can edit the same project at once with live cursors and shared terminals.

features.title: Always-on hosting
features.description: Deployments host web apps and APIs straight from the Repl with custom domains and autoscaling.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Templates and runtimes cover Python, Node.js, TypeScript, Go, Rust, and many more.

useCases: Learning to code without installing a local toolchain
Prototyping web apps and APIs and sharing live URLs
Running coding classrooms and student assignments in the browser
Building and deploying small full-stack projects with AI help
Pair-programming or interviewing remotely in a shared editor

faq.question: Is Replit free?
faq.answer: Replit has a free tier with limited compute and storage; the Core plan unlocks more resources, AI usage, and deployments.

faq.question: Can I host a real app on Replit?
faq.answer: Yes, Replit Deployments can serve web apps, APIs, and scheduled jobs with custom domains, typically on paid plans.

faq.question: Which languages does Replit support?
faq.answer: Replit supports Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, Rust, C, C++, Java, and many other languages through its Nix-based environments.

faq.question: Does Replit work offline?
faq.answer: No, Replit runs in the cloud and requires an internet connection to edit and run projects.
